description_long: This object is a detailed miniature representation of the Arc de Triomphe, a famous triumphal arch located in Paris, France. The structure features a rectangular base with two large central archways and is adorned with sculpted reliefs on its facades, depicting historical and allegorical figures. The upper section is decorated with a frieze and cornice, and there are panels that appear to be inscribed with text, mimicking the original monument's commemorative engravings. The surface has a dark, metallic patina, suggesting it is made primarily of cast metal, possibly bronze or iron. The object is compact and solid, with intricate architectural details such as pilasters and decorative motifs. The archways are open, allowing visibility and potential passage through the structure.
description: A small, dark metallic model of the Arc de Triomphe, featuring two open archways, sculpted reliefs, and inscribed panels. The surface is detailed and patinated, with classical architectural elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large archways, decorative reliefs, and inscription panels on the facade.
description_view_1: Side view revealing a single archway, less ornamentation, and the thickness of the structure.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with two archways and visible decorative details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing a single archway and minimal decoration.
